title: Kraft Heinz board approves $3 billion share buyback program
text: Kraft Heinz Co.'s KHC board has approved a share buyback program of up to $3 billion, the company said Monday. The news comes after the food company met its net leverage target of about 3.0times in the third quarter.

title: Kraft Heinz Board Authorizes Share Repurchase Program
text: PITTSBURGH & CHICAGO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Kraft Heinz Company (Nasdaq: KHC) (“Kraft Heinz” or the “Company”) today announced that the Board of Directors approved a share repurchase program authorizing the Company to repurchase up to $3 billion of the Company's outstanding shares of common stock through December 26, 2026. Under the share repurchase program, the Company intends to repurchase shares with excess cash after allocations for disciplined capital spending, including investments to supp.

title: Slow and Saucy: Heinz® unveils the Slowmaster 57 - The world's first ketchup racetrack where speed takes a backseat and true quality finishes last!
text: DUBAI, UAE, Nov. 27, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- As the world's fastest cars speed around the Yas Marina Circuit at over 200mph for the finale of the F1 season, Heinz® has proved that finishing slowest is a true sign of quality, with a saucier Ketchup Grand Prix of its own. Heinz® introduces the Slowmaster 57, the world's first ketchup racetrack ramp designed to ensure your favorite Heinz® ketchup never breaks the quality and thickness speed limit!

title: The Kraft Heinz Company (KHC) Q3 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
text: The Kraft Heinz Company (NASDAQ:KHC ) Q3 2023 Earnings Conference Call November 1, 2023 9:00 AM ET Company Participants Anne-Marie Megela - Investor Relations Miguel Patricio - Chief Executive Officer Andre Maciel - Global Chief Financial Officer Carlos Abrams-Rivera - President, North America Conference Call Participants Andrew Lazar - Barclays Peter Galbo - Bank of America John Baumgartner - Mizuho Securities Stephen Powers - Deutsche Bank Ken Goldman - JPMorgan Michael Lavery - Piper Sandler Jason English - Goldman Sachs Matt Smith - Stifel Robert Moskow - TD Cowen Operator Good day and thank you for standing by. title: Why Kraft Heinz Stock Popped Today
text: Kraft Heinz announced slightly stronger-than-expected results in the third quarter, with broad growth across its three core pillars. The global food company also made progress in improving its balance sheet, reaching a key net leverage milestone.

title: Kraft Heinz (KHC) Raises Profit Guidance on Q3 Earnings Beat
text: Kraft Heinz's (KHC) third-quarter 2023 results reflect improved earnings and sales due to higher sales in three key pillars - foodservice, emerging markets and U.S. Retail Grow platforms.

title: Kraft Heinz raises full-year profit forecast as 3Q earnings top estimates
text: Kraft Heinz Co (NASDAQ:KHC) shares moved higher in early trade as the company's third quarter earnings beat and it raised its full-year profit outlook while projecting lower sales on the lower end of its guidance range.     For 3Q, the food and beverage company reported adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $0.72, a 14.3% increase over the year-ago quarter and ahead of estimates of $0.66.

title: Kraft Heinz stock jumps after profit beat and raised outlook, while sales came up short
text: Shares of Kraft Heinz Co. KHC rallied 1.7% in premarket trading Wednesday, after the consumer food and beverage giant topped third-quarter profit expectations and lifted its full-year outlook, while sales came up a bit shy. Net income fell to $262 million, or 21 cents a share, from $432 million, or 35 cents a share, in the year-ago period.

title: The Kraft Heinz Company Declares Regular Quarterly Dividend of $0.40 Per Share

title: Kraft Heinz Reports Third Quarter 2023 Results
text: PITTSBURGH & CHICAGO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Kraft Heinz Company (Nasdaq: KHC) (“Kraft Heinz” or the “Company”) today reported financial results for the third quarter of 2023. “Our third quarter results were marked by net sales growth across each of our three core pillars: Foodservice, Emerging Markets, and U.S. Retail Grow Platforms,” said Kraft Heinz CEO and Chair of the Board Miguel Patricio. “At the same time, we continue to improve productivity across the value chain, reinvesting gross effic.
